Complete public API for Alis.Reactive. Auto-generated from XML documentation comments.
For usage and examples, see the [Grammar Tree](../../csharp-modules/mental-model/#the-grammar-tree) and the [Reactivity](../../csharp-modules/reactivity/triggers-and-reactions/) pages.

## Alis — Reactive

### ComponentRef<T>

Typed reference to a component instance on the page.
             Returned by p.Component<T>(). Vendor-specific extension methods
             constrain TComponent to add mutation methods (SetValue, Show, etc.).
            
             The ref itself is vendor-agnostic — each extension method creates a
             discriminated Mutation (set-prop, call).
             Runtime resolves vendor root via resolveRoot, then switches on mutation.kind.

```csharp
Emit()
```

### ComponentRegistration

Immutable registration of a component in the reactive plan.
            Populated at view render time by each vertical slice's HtmlExtensions.
            Consumed by GatherResolver (HTTP serialization) and ValidationResolver (field enrichment).

```csharp
// Properties
BindingPath { get; }  // Model property path (e.g., "AdmissionDate", "Address.Street") — key in ComponentsMap.
CoerceAs { get; }  // Coercion type inferred from typeof(TProp) via CoercionTypes.InferFromType at registration.             Flows to plan JSON → consumed by gather (daterange decomposition) and validation (field enrichment).             Values: "string", "number", "boolean", "date", "array", "raw".
ComponentId { get; }  // DOM element ID generated by IdGenerator from the model expression.
ComponentType { get; }  // Semantic component kind (e.g., "datepicker", "numerictextbox") — descriptive, not used for coercion.
ReadExpr { get; }  // Dot-path from vendor root to the readable value (e.g., "value", "checked").
Vendor { get; }  // "native" or "fusion" — determines resolveRoot() strategy at runtime.
```

### ExpressionPathHelper

Converts lambda expressions like `x => x.Address.City` into camelCase
            dot-paths for use as source bindings in the plan.

```csharp
ToElementId<T>(expression)
ToElementId<T>(expression)
ToEventPath<T>(expression)
ToEventPath<T>(expression)
ToPath<T>(prefix, expression)
ToPath<T>(prefix, expression)
ToPropertyName<T>(expression)
ToResponsePath<T>(expression)
```

### IAppLevelComponent

Marker for app-level components that have a well-known element ID.
            Enables the parameterless overload: p.Component<FusionConfirm>()

### IComponent

Base interface for all reactive components (Fusion SF, Native DOM).
            Every component declares its vendor ("native" or "fusion") as an instance property.
            Constrains p.Component<T>() to only accept component types.

```csharp
// Properties
Vendor { get; }  // The vendor identifier for this component ("native" or "fusion").             Determines how the runtime resolves the component root.
```

### IdGenerator

Generates collision-free HTML element IDs from model type and property expression.

```csharp
For(modelType, propertyPath)
For<T>(expression)
For<T>(expression)
TypeScope(type)
```

### IInputComponent

Interface for components that can be read — provides ReadExpr as an instance property.
            Used by gather and validation extensions as a generic constraint (with new()).

```csharp
// Properties
ReadExpr { get; }  // The property path from the vendor-determined root for reading.             Examples: "value", "checked"
```

### ReactivePlan<T>

Collects reactive behavior for a view: triggers, reactions, and component registrations.
            Renders the collected behavior so it executes in the browser.

```csharp
// Properties
ComponentsMap { get; }  // Gets the registered components keyed by model binding path.
IsPartial { get; }  // Gets whether this plan belongs to a partial view.
PlanId { get; }  // Gets the unique plan identifier, derived from the model's full type name.
```

```csharp
AddEntry()
AddToComponentsMap(bindingPath, entry)
Render()
RenderFormatted()
```

### ReactivePlanConfig

One-time startup configuration for the reactive framework.

```csharp
// Properties
Extractor { get; }  // Gets the registered validation extractor, or `null` if none is registered.
```

```csharp
Reset()
UseValidationExtractor(extractor)
```

### ResponseBody<T>

Provides typed access to HTTP response body properties in success handlers.

### TypedEventDescriptor<T>

Wraps a JS event name with a typed args instance.
             Enables C# generic inference in the .Reactive() extension:
            
               .Reactive(plan, evt => evt.Changed, (args, p) => { args.Value... })
            
             The compiler infers TArgs from the eventSelector return type.
             TArgs is a descriptor marker (e.g., FusionNumericTextBoxChangeArgs).

## Builders

### ElementBuilder<T>

Builds mutations on a DOM element: CSS classes, text content, HTML content, and visibility.

```csharp
AddClass(className)
Hide()
RemoveClass(className)
SetHtml(html)
SetHtml(source)
SetHtml<T>(source, path)
SetHtml<T>(source)
SetText(text)
SetText(source)
SetText<T>(source, path)
SetText<T>(source, path)
SetText<T>(source)
Show()
ToggleClass(className)
When<T>(payload, path, guard)
```

### ICommandEmitter

Narrow interface for emitting commands into a pipeline.
            Used by vendor-specific extensions (Fusion, Native) and ComponentRef
            instead of the full PipelineBuilder surface.

```csharp
AddCommand(command)
```

### PipelineBuilder<T>

Conditional logic: `When`/`Then`/`ElseIf`/`Else` branching and
            `Confirm` halting guards.

```csharp
Alis#Reactive#Builders#ICommandEmitter#AddCommand()
BuildReaction()
BuildReactions()
Component<T>(expr)
Component<T>(expr)
Component<T>(refId)
Component<T>()
Confirm(message)
Delete()
Dispatch(eventName)
Dispatch<T>(eventName, payload)
Element(elementId)
FlushSegment()
Get()
Into(elementId)
Parallel()
Post()
Post()
Put()
ValidationErrors(formId)
When<T>(payload, path)
When<T>(source)
```

### TriggerBuilder<T>

Wires browser triggers (page load, custom events, server-sent events, and SignalR)
            to reactions that execute in the browser.

```csharp
CustomEvent(eventName, pipeline)
CustomEvent<T>(eventName, pipeline)
DomReady(pipeline)
ServerPush(url, pipeline)
ServerPush(url, eventType, pipeline)
ServerPush<T>(url, eventType, pipeline)
SignalR(hubUrl, methodName, pipeline)
SignalR<T>(hubUrl, methodName, pipeline)
```

## Builders — Conditions

### BranchBuilder<T>

Chains additional branches after an initial `When().Then()` block using
            `ElseIf` and `Else`.

```csharp
// Properties
Pipeline { get; }  // Gets the pipeline this condition block belongs to.
```

```csharp
AddBranch()
Else(pipeline)
ElseIf<T>(payload, path)
ElseIf<T>(source)
```

### ConditionSourceBuilder<T>

Exposes typed condition operators that test a source value against a literal or
             another source. Reached by calling `When()` on the pipeline or branch builder.

```csharp
ArrayContains(item)
Between(low, high)
ComposeAndWrap()
Contains(substring)
EndsWith(suffix)
Eq(operand)
Eq(right)
Falsy()
Gt(operand)
Gt(right)
Gte(operand)
Gte(right)
In(values)
IsEmpty()
IsNull()
Lt(operand)
Lt(right)
Lte(operand)
Lte(right)
Matches(pattern)
MinLength(length)
NotEmpty()
NotEq(operand)
NotEq(right)
NotIn(values)
NotNull()
StartsWith(prefix)
Truthy()
```

### ConditionStart<T>

Entry point for building inner guards inside `And`/`Or` lambdas.

```csharp
Confirm(message)
When<T>(payload, path)
When<T>(source)
```

### EventArgSource<T>

A typed source that reads a property from the event payload at evaluation time.
            Created internally when calling `When(args, x => x.Property)`.

```csharp
ToBindSource()
```

### GuardBuilder<T>

Composes guard conditions with `And`, `Or`, and `Not`, then terminates
             with `Then` to define the commands that execute when the guard passes.

```csharp
// Properties
Guard { get; }  // Gets the guard descriptor built by the operator chain.
```

```csharp
And(inner)
And<T>(payload, path)
And<T>(source)
Not()
Or(inner)
Or<T>(payload, path)
Or<T>(source)
Then(pipeline)
```

### TypedComponentSource<T>

A typed source that reads the current value of a component in the browser.
            Returned by each component's `Value()` extension method.

```csharp
ToBindSource()
```

### TypedSource<T>

Preserves the property type through the condition and mutation pipeline so operators
            enforce compile-time type safety.

```csharp
// Properties
CoercionType { get; }  // Gets the coercion type inferred from `TProp` (e.g. `"string"`,             `"number"`, `"boolean"`).
ElementCoercionType { get; }  // Gets the element-level coercion type for array sources (e.g. `"string"` for             `string[]`). Returns `null` for non-array types.
```

```csharp
ToBindSource()
```

## Builders — Requests

### GatherBuilder<T>

Collects values from form components, event payloads, and static data to build the
            HTTP request body or URL parameters.

```csharp
AddItem()
FromEvent<T>()
IncludeAll()
Static()
```

### GatherExtensions

Vendor-agnostic gather extensions for any `IComponent` + `IInputComponent`.
            Works for both Native and Fusion components: vendor and readExpr
            are resolved from the component instance at build time.

```csharp
Include<T>()
Include<T>()
```

### HttpRequestBuilder<T>

Configures an HTTP request: URL, verb, request body (gather), loading state,
            client-side validation, and response handlers.

```csharp
AsFormData()
AsJson()
Delete(url)
Gather(gather)
Get(url)
Post(url)
Put(url)
Response(response)
Validate()
Validate<T>()
WhileLoading(pipeline)
```

### ParallelBuilder<T>

Builds a parallel HTTP reaction — multiple requests fire concurrently.
            Each branch owns its own response chain. OnAllSettled fires after all branches complete.

```csharp
OnAllSettled(pipeline)
```

### ResponseBuilder<T>

Configures success and error response handlers for an HTTP request, plus optional
            chained follow-up requests.

```csharp
BuildHandler()
Chained(request)
OnError(statusCode, pipeline)
OnSuccess(pipeline)
OnSuccess<T>()
```

---

## Descriptors

### CoercionTypes

Constants and inference logic for the `coerce` field in the JSON plan.
            The runtime uses coercion to convert string values to the correct JavaScript type.

```csharp
InferElementType()
InferFromType(type)
```

### Entry

One plan entry: a trigger paired with its reaction.
            The trigger says WHAT starts execution. The reaction says WHAT happens.

---

## Descriptors — Commands

### IntoCommand

Injects the HTTP response body as innerHTML of the target element.
            Used for loading partial views via GET requests.

### MutateEventCommand

Mutates the event args object (ctx.evt) that triggered this reaction.
             Supports both set-prop (e.g., e.preventDefaultAction = true) and
             call (e.g., e.updateData(data)).
            
             Same mutation algebra as MutateElementCommand, but the target is
             the event args object, not a DOM element. No target ID or vendor needed —
             the runtime resolves ctx.evt directly.

---

## Descriptors — Guards

### AllGuard

Logical AND combinator: all child guards must pass for the condition to be true.
            Serialized as `kind: "all"` with a `guards` array in the JSON plan.

```csharp
// Properties
Guards { get; }  // Gets the child guards that must all evaluate to `true`.
Kind { get; }  // Gets the type discriminator. Always `"all"`.
```

### AnyGuard

Logical OR combinator: at least one child guard must pass for the condition to be true.
            Serialized as `kind: "any"` with a `guards` array in the JSON plan.

```csharp
// Properties
Guards { get; }  // Gets the child guards where at least one must evaluate to `true`.
Kind { get; }  // Gets the type discriminator. Always `"any"`.
```

### ConfirmGuard

A halting guard that pauses pipeline execution and prompts the user for confirmation.
            Serialized as `kind: "confirm"` with a `message` string in the JSON plan.

```csharp
// Properties
Kind { get; }  // Gets the type discriminator. Always `"confirm"`.
Message { get; }  // Gets the message displayed to the user in the confirmation dialog.
```

### Guard

Abstract base for all guard descriptors in the JSON plan. Each concrete subclass
            serializes as a polymorphic object with a `kind` discriminator that the
            JavaScript runtime evaluates before executing a reaction or command.

### GuardOp

String constants for the `op` field in `ValueGuard`. Each constant
            maps to a comparison operator evaluated by the JavaScript runtime conditions engine.

### InvertGuard

Logical NOT wrapper: inverts the result of the `Inner` guard.
            Serialized as `kind: "not"` with a single `inner` guard in the JSON plan.

```csharp
// Properties
Inner { get; }  // Gets the child guard whose result is negated.
Kind { get; }  // Gets the type discriminator. Always `"not"`.
```

### ValueGuard

A leaf guard that evaluates a single condition: resolve the source value, coerce it,
            then apply the `Op` against an `Operand` or
            `RightSource`. Serialized as `kind: "value"` in the JSON plan.

```csharp
// Properties
CoerceAs { get; }  // Gets the coercion type applied to the source value before comparison (e.g., `"number"`, `"date"`).
ElementCoerceAs { get; }  // Gets the per-element coercion type for array operators such as             `ArrayContains`. When the source is an array, this             specifies how to coerce individual elements and the operand for             type-consistent comparison.
Kind { get; }  // Gets the type discriminator. Always `"value"`.
Op { get; }  // Gets the operator constant from `GuardOp` that defines the comparison.
Operand { get; }  // Gets the literal right-hand operand, or `null` for unary operators and source-vs-source guards.
RightSource { get; }  // Gets the right-hand source for source-vs-source comparisons. When present,             the runtime resolves this source instead of using a literal `Operand`.
Source { get; }  // Gets the left-hand source whose resolved value is tested by the operator.
```

---

## Descriptors — Reactions

### Branch

A guard-reaction pair inside a `ConditionalReaction`. If the
            `Guard` evaluates to `true` (or is `null`
            for an `Else` branch), the `Reaction` executes.

```csharp
// Properties
Guard { get; }  // Gets the guard condition for this branch, or `null` for             an unconditional `Else` fallback.
Reaction { get; }  // Gets the reaction that executes when the guard passes.
```

### ConditionalReaction

A reaction that evaluates guarded branches in order, executing the first branch
            whose guard passes. Serialized as `kind: "conditional"` in the JSON plan.

```csharp
// Properties
Branches { get; }  // Gets the ordered list of guard-reaction pairs evaluated top-to-bottom.
Commands { get; }  // Gets the unconditional commands that execute before branch evaluation, or             `null` when the reaction has no pre-condition commands.
Kind { get; }  // Gets the type discriminator. Always `"conditional"`.
```

### HttpReaction

A reaction that executes an HTTP request with optional pre-fetch commands.
            Serialized as `kind: "http"` in the JSON plan.

```csharp
// Properties
Kind { get; }  // Gets the type discriminator. Always `"http"`.
PreFetch { get; }  // Gets the commands to execute before the HTTP request, or `null`             when no pre-fetch commands exist. These commands are reverted after the response arrives.
Request { get; }  // Gets the HTTP request descriptor containing URL, method, gather items, and response handlers.
```

### SequentialReaction

A reaction that executes its commands in order. Serialized as `kind: "sequential"`
            in the JSON plan. This is the default reaction type for simple trigger-to-command pipelines.

```csharp
// Properties
Commands { get; }  // Gets the ordered list of commands to execute when the trigger fires.
Kind { get; }  // Gets the type discriminator. Always `"sequential"`.
```

---

## Descriptors — Requests

### AllGather

Plan-driven marker — runtime expands from merged plan.components at gather time.

### EventGather

Gathers a value from the event payload (execution context) at runtime.
            The path is a dot-notation walk into ctx.evt (e.g., "evt.text" → ctx.evt.text).
            Used when a trigger's event args carry the value needed for the HTTP request,
            such as the typed text from a filtering event.

```csharp
// Properties
Param { get; }  // Query parameter name (e.g., "MedicationType").
Path { get; }  // Dot-notation path into event payload (e.g., "evt.text").
```

---

## Descriptors — Sources

### BindSource

Abstract base for source binding descriptors in the JSON plan. Each concrete subclass
            serializes with a `kind` discriminator that tells the JavaScript runtime where
            to resolve a value from: an event payload or a component instance.

### ComponentSource

A source that resolves a value by reading a component instance at runtime.
            Serialized as `kind: "component"` with `componentId`, `vendor`,
            and `readExpr` fields in the JSON plan.

```csharp
// Properties
ComponentId { get; }  // Gets the DOM element ID of the component to read from.
Kind { get; }  // Gets the type discriminator. Always `"component"`.
ReadExpr { get; }  // Gets the property path walked on the resolved root to read the component value (e.g., `"value"`, `"checked"`).
Vendor { get; }  // Gets the vendor identifier (e.g., `"native"` or `"fusion"`) used to resolve the component root.
```

### EventSource

A source that resolves a value from the current event payload via a dot-path
            expression. Serialized as `kind: "event"` in the JSON plan.

```csharp
// Properties
Kind { get; }  // Gets the type discriminator. Always `"event"`.
Path { get; }  // Gets the dot-notation path into the event payload (e.g., `"evt.address.city"`).
```

---

## Descriptors — Triggers

### ComponentEventTrigger

Fires when a component's JS event fires (e.g. SF "change", DOM "click").
             Created by vendor-specific .Reactive() extensions (Fusion, Native).
            
             Vendor tells the TS runtime HOW to wire:
               fusion → el.ej2_instances[0].addEventListener(jsEvent, ...)
               native → el.addEventListener(jsEvent, ...)
            
             BindingPath is the dot-notation model property path (e.g. "Address.City")
             carried for future HTTP gather support. Optional — present when the
             component is model-bound via an expression.

```csharp
// Properties
ReadExpr { get; }  // Property path from the vendor-determined root for reading the component's value.             Examples: "value" (NativeDropDown), "checked" (NativeCheckBox).             Null for non-readable components (e.g. NativeButton).             The runtime uses walk(el, readExpr) to extract the event payload — plan-driven,             no hardcoded property lists in the runtime.
```

### ServerPushTrigger

Fires when the server sends a message via Server-Sent Events (SSE).
             Uses native browser EventSource API — zero JS library, auto-reconnect.
            
             Runtime: new EventSource(url) → addEventListener(eventType ?? "message")
             Payload: JSON.parse(event.data) → ExecContext.evt

```csharp
// Properties
EventType { get; }  // Optional SSE event type filter. When set, listens for named events             via addEventListener(eventType). When null, listens for unnamed messages             via addEventListener("message"). Maps to the "event:" field in the SSE protocol.
Url { get; }  // SSE endpoint URL (e.g. "/api/notifications/stream").
```

### SignalRTrigger

Fires when a SignalR Hub method is invoked by the server.
             Uses @microsoft/signalr client — HubConnection.on(methodName).
            
             Runtime: HubConnectionBuilder → build() → start() → on(methodName)
             Payload: Hub method arguments → ExecContext.evt
             Connection: singleton per hubUrl, shared across all triggers on same hub.

```csharp
// Properties
HubUrl { get; }  // Hub URL (e.g. "/hubs/notifications"). Connection is shared             across all triggers with the same hubUrl.
MethodName { get; }  // Server method name to listen for (e.g. "ReceiveNotification").             Maps to connection.on(methodName, handler).
```

---

## InputField

### InputBoundFieldBase<T>

Framework-agnostic base for a model-bound input field.

```csharp
// Properties
BindingPath { get; }  // Gets the model binding path (e.g. `"Address.City"`) for validation message targeting.
ElementId { get; }  // Gets the generated HTML element ID for this field's input.
Expression { get; }  // Gets the model property expression this field is bound to.
Helper { get; }  // Gets the platform-specific HTML helper for rendering.
Options { get; }  // Gets the label and required options for this field.
Plan { get; }  // Gets the plan this field belongs to.
Writer { get; }  // Gets the writer for emitting HTML output.
```

```csharp
Render()
```

### InputFieldBuilder

Renders a field wrapper: `<div>`, label with optional required marker,
            inner content slot, and validation error placeholder.

```csharp
Begin()
ForId()
Label()
Required()
```

### InputFieldOptions

Configures the label and required marker for an input field wrapper.

```csharp
// Properties
IsRequired { get; }  // Gets whether the required marker (`*`) should be shown.
LabelText { get; }  // Gets the label text, or `null` if no label was configured.
```

```csharp
Label(label)
Required()
```

### InputFieldRenderScope

Writes closing HTML tags when disposed. Pure BCL.

---

## Resolvers

### ValidationResolver

Walks the reaction tree and resolves validation rules from an IValidationExtractor.
            After extraction, enriches validation fields from the ComponentsMap.

---

## Validation

### IValidationExtractor

Extracts client-side validation rules from a validator type.
            Implemented by FluentValidationAdapter.

### ValidationCondition

A simple condition for conditional validation rules.
            Operators: "truthy", "falsy", "eq", "neq".

```csharp
// Properties
Field { get; }  // Property name to check (e.g. "IsEmployed").
Op { get; }  // Operator: "truthy", "falsy", "eq", "neq".
Value { get; }  // Comparison value for "eq" and "neq" operators.
```

### ValidationDescriptor

Describes all validation rules for a single form.
            Serialized to JSON and consumed by the TS runtime validation engine.

```csharp
// Properties
PlanId { get; }  // Plan identity — used by runtime to scope summary div lookup.
```

### ValidationField

Describes a single field's validation rules within a form.
            Enriched at C# render time from ComponentsMap, then at TS boot from plan.components.

```csharp
// Properties
CoerceAs { get; }  // Coercion type from component registration (e.g. "date", "number"). Null when unenriched.
FieldId { get; }  // Element ID from component registration. Null when unenriched.
FieldName { get; }  // Model property name using dot notation (e.g. "Address.Street").
ReadExpr { get; }  // Property path to read value (e.g. "value", "checked"). Null when unenriched.
Vendor { get; }  // Vendor string ("native" or "fusion"). Null when unenriched.
```

### ValidationRule

A single validation rule (one of 18 types: required, empty, minLength, maxLength,
            email, regex, url, creditCard, range, exclusiveRange, min, max, gt, lt,
            equalTo, notEqual, notEqualTo, atLeastOne).

```csharp
// Properties
CoerceAs { get; }  // Coercion type for comparison — derived from TProperty at extraction time.             "number" for numeric types, "date" for DateTime/DateOnly, omitted for string comparison.
Constraint { get; }  // Rule constraint value. Type depends on rule:             number (minLength, maxLength, min, max), string (regex, notEqual),             [value,value] (range, exclusiveRange), field name (equalTo, notEqualTo).             Null when not applicable.
Field { get; }  // Cross-property binding path — when present, the comparison target is read             from another field instead of using a fixed constraint value.             Mutually exclusive with Constraint for comparison rules (min, max, gt, lt, equalTo, notEqualTo).
Message { get; }  // Error message displayed when validation fails.
Rule { get; }  // Rule type string (e.g. "required", "minLength").
When { get; }  // Optional condition — rule only applies when condition is met.             Used for conditional validation (e.g. "required when IsEmployed is truthy").
```

---

## Fusion

### FusionComponent

Base type for all Syncfusion-backed components.

```csharp
// Properties
Vendor { get; }  // 
```

---

## Fusion — AppLevel

### FusionConfirm

App-level confirm dialog backed by Syncfusion Dialog.
             Singleton per page — one SF Dialog instance serves all ConfirmGuard evaluations.
            
             Implements IAppLevelComponent so it can be resolved without an explicit ID:
               p.Component<FusionConfirm>().Show()

### FusionToast

App-level toast notification backed by Syncfusion Toast.
             Singleton per page — one SF Toast instance serves all notifications.
            
             Implements IAppLevelComponent so it can be resolved without an explicit ID:
               p.Component<FusionToast>().SetContent("Saved").Success().Show()

### ToastPosition

Maps to Syncfusion Toast position { X, Y } values.

### ToastType

Maps to Syncfusion Toast cssClass values.

---

## Fusion — Components

### FusionAccordion

FusionAccordion — non-input container component.
            Has events and methods but no form value (no ReadExpr, no IInputComponent).

### FusionAccordionBuilder<T>

Wraps SF AccordionBuilder.Render() output + carries plan and elementId
            for .Reactive() chaining. Non-input component — no ComponentsMap registration.

### FusionAccordionEvents

Events available on FusionAccordion.
            Singleton instance — used with .Reactive() event selector lambda:
              .Reactive(evt => evt.Expanded, (args, p) => { ... })

```csharp
// Properties
Expanded { get; }  // Fires after a panel expands or collapses (SF "expanded" event).
```

### FusionAccordionExpandedArgs

Payload for FusionAccordion.Expanded (SF "expanded" event).
            Fires after a panel expands or collapses.
            Properties are typed markers for expression-based condition sources:
              p.When(args, x => x.IsExpanded).Truthy()
            ExpressionPathHelper resolves x => x.IsExpanded to "evt.isExpanded".

```csharp
// Properties
Index { get; }  // The zero-based index of the panel that was expanded/collapsed.
IsExpanded { get; }  // True if the panel was expanded, false if collapsed.
```

### FusionAccordionExtensions

Mutation extensions for `FusionAccordion` in a reactive pipeline.

```csharp
EnableItem<T>()
ExpandItem<T>()
```

### FusionAccordionHtmlExtensions

Factory extension for creating FusionAccordionBuilder.
            Non-input component — NO InputField wrapper, NO ComponentsMap registration.

```csharp
FusionAccordion<T>()
```

### FusionAccordionReactiveExtensions

Wires browser events from a `FusionAccordion` into the reactive plan.

### FusionAutoComplete

A FusionAutoComplete for typing and filtering suggestions from a data source.

```csharp
// Properties
ReadExpr { get; }  // 
```

### FusionAutoCompleteChangeArgs

Event payload delivered when a `FusionAutoComplete` selection changes.

```csharp
// Properties
IsInteracted { get; }  // Gets or sets whether the change was triggered by user interaction.
Value { get; }  // Gets or sets the selected value.
```

### FusionAutoCompleteEvents

Typed event descriptors for the `FusionAutoComplete` component.

```csharp
// Properties
Changed { get; }  // Fires when the selected value changes (SF "change" event).
Filtering { get; }  // Fires when the user types to filter (SF "filtering" event).
```

### FusionAutoCompleteExtensions

Typed mutations and value reading for `FusionAutoComplete` in a reactive pipeline.

### FusionTab

FusionTab (ej2.navigations.Tab) component.
            Non-input component — container with no form value.
            No ReadExpr, no ComponentsMap registration, no validation, no gather.

### FusionTabBuilder<T>

Wrapper builder for the FusionTab component.
            Wraps SF-rendered IHtmlContent and exposes ElementId + Plan for .Reactive() chaining.
            Non-input component — no InputField wrapper, no label, no validation slot.

```csharp
// Properties
ElementId { get; }  // The element ID — used by .Reactive() to wire events.
Plan { get; }  // The reactive plan — used by .Reactive() to add entries.
```

### FusionTabEvents

Events available on FusionTab.
            Singleton instance — used with .Reactive() event selector lambda:
              .Reactive(evt => evt.Selected, (args, p) => { ... })

```csharp
// Properties
Selected { get; }  // Fires when a tab is selected (SF "selected" event).
```

### FusionTabExtensions

Mutation extensions for `FusionTab` in a reactive pipeline.

```csharp
HideTab<T>()
Select<T>()
SetSelectedItem<T>()
```

### FusionTabHtmlExtensions

Factory extension for creating FusionTab (non-input component).
            No InputField wrapper. No ComponentsMap registration.
            Uses explicit string elementId (not model-expression-derived).

```csharp
FusionTab<T>()
```

### FusionTabReactiveExtensions

Wires browser events from a `FusionTab` into the reactive plan.

### FusionTabSelectedArgs

Event args for the SF Tab "selected" event.
             Fires when a tab is selected.
            
             Properties are primitives — safe for echo and conditions.
             SF args also contain selectedItem/previousItem (DOM objects) — intentionally omitted
             as they would serialize as [object Object].

```csharp
// Properties
IsSwiped { get; }  // Whether the selection was triggered by a swipe gesture.
PreviousIndex { get; }  // The zero-based index of the previously selected tab.
SelectedIndex { get; }  // The zero-based index of the newly selected tab.
```

### TestWidgetSyncFusion

Test widget for architecture verification — fusion vendor.
            Phantom type — proves vendor resolves root via ej2_instances[0],
            and readExpr walks from that root.

```csharp
// Properties
ReadExpr { get; }  // 
```

### TestWidgetSyncFusionBuilder<T>

Renders a <div data-test-widget> element that auto-mounts as a TestWidget
             with ej2_instances pattern. Not model-bound — for standalone test widgets.
            
             Usage:
               @Html.TestWidget("my-widget")
                   .InitialValue("hello")
                   .CssClass("rounded-md border border-border px-3 py-1.5 text-sm w-full")

```csharp
// Properties
ElementId { get; }  // The element ID — used by .Reactive() to wire events.
```

```csharp
CssClass()
InitialValue()
```

### TestWidgetSyncFusionHtmlExtensions

Factory extension for creating TestWidgetSyncFusionBuilder.

```csharp
TestWidget<T>()
```

---

## Native

### InputBoundField<T>

A model-bound input field returned by
            `InputFieldOptions})`, ready to receive
            a component extension that renders inside the field wrapper.

### NativeComponent

Base class for all native HTML components (text inputs, checkboxes, dropdowns, etc.).

```csharp
// Properties
Vendor { get; }  // 
```

---

## Native — AppLevel

### DrawerPosition

The edge of the viewport that the `NativeDrawer` slides in from.

### DrawerSize

Controls the width of the `NativeDrawer` panel.

### NativeDrawer

App-level slide-out drawer panel.

```csharp
// Properties
DefaultId { get; }  // 
```

### NativeDrawerExtensions

Pipeline and layout extensions for the `IHtmlHelper)`.

```csharp
Close<T>()
NativeDrawer()
Open<T>()
SetSize<T>(self, size)
```

### NativeLoader

App-level loading overlay that covers its target container or the viewport.

```csharp
// Properties
DefaultId { get; }  // 
```

### NativeLoaderExtensions

Pipeline and layout extensions for the `IHtmlHelper)`.

```csharp
Hide<T>()
NativeLoader()
SetTarget<T>(self, targetId)
SetTimeout<T>(self, ms)
Show<T>()
```

---

## Native — Components

### NativeButton

Native HTML <button> element.
            Phantom type — constrains which vertical slice extensions are available.
            Not an IInputComponent — buttons have no form value to read.

### NativeButtonBuilder<T>

Renders a native HTML <button> element with an explicit id.
             Not model-bound — buttons don't carry data.
            
             Usage:
               @Html.NativeButton("save-btn", "Save")
                   .CssClass("rounded-md bg-accent px-4 py-2 text-sm font-medium text-white")
                   .Reactive(plan, evt => evt.Click, (args, p) =>
                   {
                       p.Post("/api/save", g => g.Static("name", "John"))
                        .Response(r => r.OnSuccess(s => ...));
                   })
            
             .Reactive() is always the last call — native builders are IHtmlContent
             directly (no .Render() needed).

```csharp
// Properties
ElementId { get; }  // The element ID — used by .Reactive() to wire events.
```

```csharp
CssClass()
Type()
```

### NativeButtonClickArgs

Payload for NativeButton.Click (DOM "click" event).
            Button clicks carry no data payload — this class is an empty marker
            used by the generic inference chain in .Reactive().

### NativeButtonEvents

Events available on NativeButton.
            Singleton instance — used with .Reactive() event selector lambda:
              .Reactive(plan, evt => evt.Click, (args, p) => { ... })

```csharp
// Properties
Click { get; }  // Fires when the user clicks the button (DOM "click" event).
```

### NativeButtonHtmlExtensions

Factory extension for creating NativeButtonBuilder.

```csharp
NativeButton<T>()
```

### NativeButtonReactiveExtensions

Wires reactive event pipelines onto the NativeButtonBuilder.
            
             Usage (in .cshtml):
               @Html.NativeButton("save-btn", "Save")
                   .CssClass("...")
                   .Reactive(plan, evt => evt.Click, (args, p) =>
                   {
                       p.Post("/api/save", g => g.Static("name", "John"))
                        .Response(r => r.OnSuccess(s => s.Element("result").SetText("Saved!")));
                   })
            
             .Reactive() is always the last call — the builder implements IHtmlContent.

### NativeCheckBox

A native HTML checkbox (`<input type="checkbox">`).

```csharp
// Properties
ReadExpr { get; }  // 
```

Configures and renders a native HTML `<input type="checkbox">` bound to a model property.

```csharp
// Properties
BindingPath { get; }  // Gets the model binding path (e.g. `"IsActive"`).
ElementId { get; }  // Gets the resolved element ID for this checkbox.
```

```csharp
CssClass(css)
WriteTo()
```

### NativeCheckBoxChangeArgs

Event args for `Changed`.

```csharp
Checked { get; }  // Gets or sets the checked state after the change.
```


```csharp
SetChecked<T>(self, isChecked)
Value<T>()
```

```csharp
Options { get; }  // Gets the configured checkbox options.
```

```csharp
CssClass(css)
Items(items)
Option(value)
Option(value, text)
Option(value, text, description)
OptionCssClass(css)
WriteTo()
```

```csharp
NativeCheckList<T>(setup, build)
```

### NativeCheckListReactiveExtensions

Wires browser events from `NativeCheckList` into the reactive plan.

```csharp
Reactive<T>(builder, plan, eventSelector, pipeline)
```

### NativeDropDown

A native HTML dropdown (`<select>`).

```csharp
// Properties
ReadExpr { get; }  // 
```

### NativeDropDownBuilder<T>

Configures and renders a native HTML `<select>` element bound to a model property.

```csharp
// Properties
BindingPath { get; }  // Gets the model binding path (e.g. `"Address.City"`).
ElementId { get; }  // Gets the resolved element ID for this dropdown.
```

```csharp
CssClass(css)
Enabled(enabled)
Items(items)
Placeholder(optionLabel)
WriteTo()
```

### NativeDropDownChangeArgs

Event args for `Changed`.

```csharp
// Properties
Value { get; }  // Gets or sets the selected option's value after the change.
```

### NativeDropDownEvents

Typed event descriptors for `NativeDropDown`.

```csharp
// Properties
Changed { get; }  // Fires when the user selects a different option.
```

### NativeDropDownExtensions

Pipeline extensions for `NativeDropDown`: set selected value, focus, and read.

```csharp
FocusIn<T>()
SetValue<T>(self, value)
Value<T>()
```

### NativeDropDownHtmlExtensions

Factory extension for creating a `NativeDropDownBuilder{<T>` inside a field wrapper.

```csharp
NativeDropDown<T>(setup, build)
```

### NativeDropDownReactiveExtensions

Wires browser events from `NativeDropDown` into the reactive plan.

### NativeHiddenField

Native HTML <input type="hidden"> element.
            Phantom type — constrains which vertical slice extensions are available.
            Participates in ComponentsMap for gather (IncludeAll picks it up).

```csharp
// Properties
ReadExpr { get; }  // 
```

### NativeHiddenFieldBuilder<T>

Renders a native HTML <input type="hidden"> element bound to a model property.
            Uses IdGenerator for element ID and MVC NameFor for the name attribute.
            No label, no validation slot — hidden inputs are invisible.

### NativeHiddenFieldChangeArgs

Payload for NativeHiddenField.Changed (DOM "change" event).
            Properties are typed markers for expression-based condition sources:
              p.When(args, x => x.Value).Eq("RES-1042")
            ExpressionPathHelper resolves x => x.Value to "evt.value".

```csharp
// Properties
Value { get; }  // The hidden input's value after the change.
```

### NativeHiddenFieldEvents

Events available on NativeHiddenField.
            Singleton instance — used with .Reactive() event selector lambda:
              .Reactive(plan, evt => evt.Changed, (args, p) => { ... })
            Hidden inputs rarely fire change events — this exists for completeness.

```csharp
// Properties
Changed { get; }  // Fires when the hidden input value changes (DOM "change" event).
```

### NativeHiddenFieldHtmlExtensions

Factory extension for creating NativeHiddenFieldBuilder.
            Hidden fields bypass InputField wrapper entirely — no label, no validation slot.
            Registers in ComponentsMap for gather (IncludeAll picks it up).

```csharp
HiddenFieldFor<T>()
```

### NativeHiddenFieldReactiveExtensions

Wires reactive event pipelines onto the NativeHiddenFieldBuilder.
            Hidden inputs rarely fire change events — this exists for completeness
            (programmatic value changes can be observed via dispatched change events).

```csharp
Options { get; }  // Gets the configured radio options.
```

```csharp
Items(items)
Option(value)
Option(value, text)
Option(value, text, description)
OptionCssClass(css)
```

### RadioButtonItem

A single radio or checkbox option with a value, display text, and optional description.

```csharp
// Properties
Description { get; }  // Gets the optional secondary description shown below the display text.
Text { get; }  // Gets the display text shown next to the radio button or checkbox.
Value { get; }  // Gets the option value submitted in the form.
```

### TestWidgetNative

Test widget for architecture verification — native vendor.
            Phantom type — proves the same readExpr works for both vendors.

```csharp
// Properties
ReadExpr { get; }  // 
```

### TestWidgetNativeBuilder<T>

Renders an <input type="text"> element for architecture verification.
             Not model-bound — for standalone native test inputs.
            
             Usage:
               @(Html.TestWidgetNative<TModel>("my-input")
                   .InitialValue("hello")
                   .CssClass("rounded-md border"))

```csharp
// Properties
ElementId { get; }  // The element ID — used by .Reactive() to wire events.
```

```csharp
CssClass()
InitialValue()
ReadOnly()
```

### TestWidgetNativeChangeArgs

Payload for TestWidgetNative.Changed (DOM "change" event).
            Properties are typed markers for expression-based source binding:
              p.Element("result").SetText(args, x => x.Value)
            ExpressionPathHelper resolves x => x.Value to "evt.value".

```csharp
// Properties
Value { get; }  // The input's value after the change.
```

### TestWidgetNativeEvents

Events available on TestWidgetNative.
            Singleton instance — used with .Reactive() event selector lambda:
              .Reactive(plan, evt => evt.Changed, (args, p) => { ... })

```csharp
// Properties
Changed { get; }  // Fires when the user changes the input value (DOM "change" event).
```

### TestWidgetNativeHtmlExtensions

Factory extension for creating TestWidgetNativeBuilder.

```csharp
TestWidgetNative<T>()
```

---

## Native — Extensions

### HtmlExtensions

Razor view extensions for adding reactive behavior to a plan.

```csharp
On<T>(html, plan, trigger)
```

### InputFieldExtensions

Razor view extension for starting a model-bound input field.

```csharp
InputField<T>(html, plan, expression, options)
```

### NativeGatherExtensions

Gather shorthand that defaults to `NativeTextBox` when no component
            type is specified.

```csharp
Include<T>(self, expr)
```

### PlanExtensions

Razor view extensions that open and close every reactive view: create the
            `IHtmlHelper{<T>` at the top, render it at the bottom.

```csharp
ReactivePlan<T>()
RenderPlan<T>(html, plan)
ResolvePlan<T>()
```

---
